questions.question: Given two 8‑bit numbers in 2’s complement form, A = 01101010 and B = 11010101, perform binary addition and determine whether an overflow occurs. Show the steps and state the overflow condition for 2’s complement addition.
keyPoints: Perform bit‑wise addition of A and B, including carry
Identify result and any final carry
Overflow condition: carry into MSB XOR carry out of MSB (for signed addition)
Indicate whether overflow occurred (yes/no) with justification

questions.question: Write the Register Transfer Language (RTL) notation for a microoperation that adds the contents of register R1 to register R2 and stores the result in register R3, using 2’s complement representation. Also specify the condition under which overflow is flagged.
keyPoints: Indicate the use of addition microoperation (e.g., ADD, ADDC)
Overflow flag set when carry into sign bit ≠ carry out of sign bit

questions.question: Design a simple n‑bit Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) that can execute the following microoperations: (i) addition (A + B), (ii) subtraction (A – B) using 2’s complement, (iii) increment (A + 1), and (iv) pass‑through A. Provide the functional table, describe the selection bits, and explain how overflow detection is performed for the addition and subtraction operations.
keyPoints: Define selection bits S1, S0 to choose operation (00=pass A, 01=increment, 10=addition, 11=subtraction)
Use a ripple‑carry adder with a subtractor realized by adding B’s 2’s complement (inverted B + 1)
Overflow detection: XOR of carry into sign bit and carry out of sign bit for add/sub
Show simplified circuit block diagram (adder, inverter, mux) conceptually

questions.question: Explain the process of BCD (Binary‑Coded Decimal) addition with a numerical example. Describe the steps when the sum of two BCD digits exceeds 9, and discuss the correction method (add 6) and its implementation using a binary adder and a detection circuit.
keyPoints: Perform binary addition of the two BCD digits
Add binary 0110 (6) to the result to get correct BCD digit
Show example e.g., 0111 (7) + 1001 (9) = 10000 → after add 6 = 0001 0110 (16 decimal) representing 1 and 6
